circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track designed by Hermann Tilke on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island, has been the home of Formula One's season-concluding Abu Dhabi Grand Prix since 2009. The venue represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One circuit, following Bahrain and preceding those built in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: Albert Park Circuit is a street course encircling a lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb that hosts the annual Australian Grand Prix and other racing events. The track maintains FIA Grade 1 certification and serves as the venue for multiple racing series including Supercars competition.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Styria, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit was originally established as the Österreichring and staged the Austrian Grand Prix continuously from 1970 to 1987. Following reconstruction and shortening, the track was renamed and again hosted the Austrian Grand Prix from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street racing venue in Azerbaijan's capital, positioned alongside Baku Boulevard, and at 6. 003 km per lap ranks among the longest tracks used in Formula One competition. The circuit first hosted a major race in 2016 with the European Grand Prix, before transitioning to the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onward, with operations managed by the Baku City Circuit Operation Company.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, Bahrain International Circuit is a 5. 412 km venue that hosted the Middle East's inaugural Formula One Grand Prix and continues to hold the annual Bahrain Grand Prix. The FIA Grade 1-licensed track accommodates various motorsport categories including GP2 racing, drag racing, and 24-hour endurance competitions, with multiple circuit configurations available.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657-kilometre Spanish circuit features a balanced combination of lengthy straight sections and diverse corner configurations, making it a versatile test for all-round racing performance. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 status and accommodates up to 140,700 spectators across its viewing areas.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Located in the Walloon region of Belgium near the town of Spa, this 7. 004 km circuit has served as the Belgian Grand Prix venue since 1925 and participated in Formula One's inaugural season in 1950. The track has maintained an annual Grand Prix presence since 1985, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, Quebec, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a 4. 361 km temporary street circuit that serves as the home of the Canadian Grand Prix in Formula One. Beyond its Formula One commitment, the track has accommodated numerous major motorsport championships including sports car racing series and North American open-wheel competitions.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit in China's Jiading District hosted the Chinese Grand Prix from 2004 to 2019 before resuming the event from 2024 onward. The venue also began hosting the Shanghai ePrix electric racing championship starting in 2024.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located in the English Midlands near Silverstone and Whittlebury, this circuit has hosted the British Grand Prix since 1948 and was the venue for the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After sharing hosting duties with Aintree and Brands Hatch for three decades, the venue became the permanent home of Britain's premier motorsport event from 1987 onward.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring near Budapest became the first Formula One circuit to host a Grand Prix behind the Iron Curtain when it opened in 1986. Built in just eight months outside the city following government preference over a proposed Monaco-style street circuit in a Budapest park, the 4. 381 km track held its inaugural race on 24 March 1986 as a tribute to Hungarian motorcycle racing pioneer János Drapál.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Located near Monza in northern Italy, the Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km circuit constructed in 1922 as Europe's oldest purpose-built racing venue. The track has hosted the Italian Grand Prix annually since 1949, interrupted only in 1980 for renovations, and earned the nickname "Temple of Speed" for its characteristic long straights and fast-cornering design.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Located in Mie Prefecture, Japan, Suzuka International Racing Course is a 5. 807 km track operated by Honda Mobilityland that hosts major motorsport events including Formula One and Japanese Super Formula championships. The venue can accommodate 155,000 spectators and has established itself as one of the sport's most prominent racing destinations.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, incorporating the famous Las Vegas Boulevard and its iconic venues. The track weaves past major casinos and hotels such as Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas, as well as the modern MSG Sphere entertainment complex. This urban layout creates a racing venue centered on the city's most recognizable attractions and landmarks.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is a 4. 304 km circuit honoring racing brothers Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both of whom died in racing accidents in the 1960s and 1970s. The venue returned to hosting the Formula One Mexican Grand Prix in 2015 after a 23-year absence, having previously held the event on a different configuration until 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, Florida, measuring 3. 363 miles with 19 corners and designed specifically for Formula One racing by specialist track architects. The venue joined the F1 calendar in 2022 as the home of the Miami Grand Prix, where cars typically achieve qualifying speeds near 139 mph.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Monaco circuit covers 3. 337 kilometres through the streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine, following routes around the principality's harbour. Though technically situated across multiple districts, it is widely known as the Monte Carlo circuit due to the majority of its layout occupying that particular neighbourhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: This 4. 259-kilometer Dutch motorsport facility sits in the coastal dunes near Amsterdam and hosted the revived Dutch Grand Prix starting in 2021, though the Formula One partnership concludes after 2026. The track, positioned just inland from the North Sea, has carried various commercial names throughout its history, most recently adopting a sponsorship designation in 2025.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit is situated near Lusail in Qatar, north of Doha, and features a 5. 419-kilometer layout with a 1. 068-kilometer main straight.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos, officially the Autódromo José Carlos Pace, is a 4. 309 km racing circuit in São Paulo, Brazil, opened in 1940 and renamed in 1985 to commemorate the late Formula One driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in an aviation accident eight years earlier. The track operates in a counterclockwise direction and features an adjacent kart facility dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street racing venue situated along Saudi Arabia's Red Sea coastline that has hosted the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since its debut in 2021 and began accommodating Formula E races from 2025 onwards.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's downtown waterfront area, utilizing public roads around the bay to create a challenging urban racing venue.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located near Barcelona in Catalonia, Spain, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya stretches 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced design combining extended straightaways with diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing multiple racing disciplines. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and can accommodate approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile international racing facility that serves as the permanent home of Formula One's United States Grand Prix. Beyond F1, the track hosts multiple major motorsport events including MotoGP's Americas round and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race, alongside various other series across different racing disciplines.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai and British racing driver who represents Thailand in Formula One competition while driving for Williams.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso currently races for Aston Martin in Formula One, where he has claimed two world championships during his 23-season career alongside 32 Grand Prix victories. Beyond single-seaters, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance motorsport, including two Le Mans 24 Hours victories and the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to have won both the F1 drivers' title and the world endurance drivers' championship.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli represents Mercedes in Formula One, having secured five race victories following his series debut in 2025.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British driver currently racing in Formula One with the Haas team.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabi Bortoleto competes in Formula One for Audi, where he races under his full name Gabriel Lourenzo Bortoleto Oliveira.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: The Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas currently races for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing considerable experience from a 13-season career that has yielded 10 race victories. He previously competed for Mercedes, where he secured two runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship during 2019 and 2020.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Colapinto is an Argentine driver currently competing in Formula One with Alpine, bringing representation from South America to the grid.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan is an Australian driver who races in European endurance competitions while holding a reserve role at Haas in Formula One. Between 2024 and 2025, he gained limited Formula One experience by competing in seven Grand Prix events.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. Previously competing for AlphaTauri, he achieved his sole Grand Prix victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Hadjar is a driver of French and Algerian descent who races for Red Bull Racing in Formula One, competing under the French flag.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British driver currently racing for Ferrari who has secured seven world championship titles, matching Michael Schumacher's record. He holds multiple Formula One records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races for Audi in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from his motorsport career. Beyond single-seaters, he achieved significant success in endurance competition, claiming victory at the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 with Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand-born driver currently racing in Formula One with Racing Bulls, competing at the highest level of international motorsport.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc, a Monégasque driver competing for Ferrari, achieved second place in the 2022 drivers' championship and has secured nine Grand Prix victories throughout his nine seasons in Formula One.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen, a Danish driver, raced in Formula One during two separate periods spanning 2014-2020 and 2022-2024. Currently, he competes in the World Endurance Championship as a factory driver for BMW with WRT, while also pursuing a part-time NASCAR career with Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver competing for McLaren, claimed the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship with the team. Across his eight seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 11 Grand Prix victories.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. The skilled competitor achieved a notable victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while driving for Alpine earlier in his career.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Mexican driver Sergio "Checo" Pérez competes for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing over 15 seasons of experience and six Grand Prix victories to the grid. He finished as championship runner-up in 2023 while driving for Red Bull.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri, an Australian driver currently racing for McLaren, has secured nine Grand Prix victories throughout his four-year Formula One career.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo, an Australian driver known as "the Honey Badger," competed in Formula One for 14 seasons between 2011 and 2024, securing eight race victories during his career in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell currently races for Mercedes in Formula One, having accumulated seven Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-season career in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz currently races for Williams in Formula One, bringing experience accumulated over 12 seasons in the sport. He has achieved four Grand Prix victories during his career in the championship.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver currently racing in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition. After competing in Formula One during 2023 and 2024, he has transitioned to sports car racing, including prior experience in IMSA's LMP2 class with Era Motorsport.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Canadian driver Lance Stroll currently races for Aston Martin in Formula One competition.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Japanese driver Yuki Tsunoda participated in Formula One racing between 2021 and 2025, currently holding a reserve driver position with both Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: The Dutch-Belgian driver Max Verstappen has secured four consecutive World Drivers' Championship titles between 2021 and 2024 while competing for Red Bull Racing. Across his 12-season Formula One career, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu, the sole Chinese driver to have participated in Formula One, raced in the championship between 2022 and 2024 before transitioning to a reserve role with Cadillac.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: Alpine Racing competes as Renault's works team from its Enstone base, having been rebranded under the Alpine sports car marque starting in 2021 after competing as Renault F1 Team. The squad secured a single victory during its Alpine era when Esteban Ocon triumphed at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, before Renault's withdrawal from the sport at the end of 2025.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British automotive manufacturer, has competed in Formula One across multiple incarnations, with its current racing operation run through AMR GP Limited under the Aston Martin Aramco sponsorship banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker Ferrari, stands as Formula One's longest-running and most accomplished team, participating in every World Championship since the sport's inception in 1950. The squad, nicknamed "the Prancing Horse" after their iconic emblem, remains a foundational institution in grand prix racing.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: TGR Haas F1 Team is an American Formula One outfit founded by NASCAR team owner Gene Haas that entered the grid in 2016. The squad has been led since early 2024 by team principal Ayao Komatsu, succeeding Guenther Steiner who held the position since the team's establishment.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: A Swiss engineering and racing enterprise established by Peter Sauber in 1970, the organization initially competed in sports car racing before transitioning to Formula One from 1993 onwards. The team secured notable endurance racing achievements, including victory at Le Mans in 1989 alongside Mercedes-Benz, though its single-seater program saw numerous transformations across three decades before ceasing operations in 2025. Audi acquired the organization in 2024 to serve as the technological and competitive foundation for its own Formula One venture.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren is a British Formula One outfit headquartered in Woking that operates as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group. The team currently races under title sponsorship and competes from the McLaren Technology Centre facility in Surrey.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: The Mercedes-AMG Petronas Formula One Team, operating from Brackley with a German licence, represents the modern continuation of Mercedes-Benz's involvement in Formula One dating back to 1954. From January 2022, ownership became shared equally among Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and team principal Toto Wolff. The team carries forward the legendary "Silver Arrows" heritage associated with Mercedes racing programmes.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team that began competing in 2024 as one of two constructors operated by Red Bull GmbH. Based in Faenza with a secondary facility in Milton Keynes, the team functions alongside its sister outfit Red Bull Racing within the Red Bull motorsport portfolio.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One constructor that joined the grid in 2024, operating as the second team under Red Bull GmbH's ownership alongside Red Bull Racing. Headquartered in Faenza with an additional facility near its sister team in Milton Keynes, the squad competes as a separate constructor entity within the Red Bull organization.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, based in England but competing under an Austrian licence, emerged as a Formula One team in 2005 and became one of two series entries owned by Red Bull GmbH, the other being Racing Bulls. Christian Horner led the team from its inception until early 2025, when Laurent Mekies assumed the leadership role.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering, operating as Atlassian Williams F1 Team in 2026, is a British constructor founded by Frank Williams and Patrick Head in 1977, emerging from Williams's previous failed racing venture. Based in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team has established itself as a significant presence in Formula One competition.
